key_assumptions.input: Starting free cash flow per share
key_assumptions.source: FY2025 after-tax operating income $3700.0M ÷ 375.9M shares = $9.84/sh; excludes net realized gains per 10-K non-GAAP definition
Mid-single-digit premium growth, combined ratios normalize, buybacks continue; 11× exit on year-10 owner cash

open_questions: Normalized owner cash uses after-tax operating income, not GAAP EPS
Mortgage combined ratio **14.6%** may not persist through housing downturn
No approved third-party sources in base IRR
thesis: Arch Capital Group Ltd. (NASDAQ: **ACGL**) is a Bermuda-domiciled specialty insurer, reinsurer, and mortgage insurer operating through three segments: Insurance, Reinsurance, and Mortgage. **[Fact]** FY2025 net income available to Arch common shareholders was **$4.4 billion** and book value per share was **$65.11** (up from **$53.11** in FY2024). **[Fact]** After-tax operating income (the company's normalized earnings measure, excluding net realized gains) was **$3.7 billion**, or about **$9.84 per diluted share** on **375.9 million** weighted average diluted shares. **[Fact]** The group wrote **$2.9 billion** of underwriting income across segments in FY2025, supplemented by **$1.6 billion** of net investment income. **[Inference]** Arch is a capital-light compounder in global specialty risk: it earns underwriting margin where expertise creates pricing power, invests float at roughly **4%** pre-tax yield, and returns excess capital through repurchases.
why_market_wrong: There is **no classic dated mispricing signal** (HK predictive attribute: **none**). **[Inference]** Generalists may anchor on GAAP net income (**$11.60** diluted earnings per share) that includes realized investment gains and equity-method marks, overstating repeatable owner cash versus after-tax operating income of **$9.84 per share**. **[Fact]** The stock trades near **1.6 times** December 2025 book value (**$65.11**), embedding continued strong reinsurance and mortgage profitability. **[Fact]** Insurance segment combined ratio was **95.2%** in FY2025, near breakeven underwriting, while reinsurance (**80.8%**) and mortgage (**14.6%**) carry the group. **[Inference]** The market may be paying for mid-cycle peak margins in reinsurance and mortgage without fully reserving for property catastrophe clustering, social inflation, or a housing downturn. Return here is **normalized owner cash at price**, not a simple price-to-book trade.
